title: Error Handling and Retry Logic
class_hierarchy: BinlogHeartbeatErrorHandler implements HeartbeatErrorHandler — inner class of BinlogSourceTask
HeartbeatErrorHandler (interface) — in io.debezium.heartbeat, has DEFAULT_NOOP_ERRORHANDLER lambda
key_fields: ErrorHandler.queue: ChangeEventQueue<?> — target for exception propagation
ErrorHandler.producerThrowable: AtomicReference<Throwable> — stores first producer error
ErrorHandler.maxRetries: int — set from connectorConfig.getMaxRetriesOnError(); RETRIES_UNLIMITED=-1, RETRIES_DISABLED=0
ErrorHandler.retries: int — current retry count, transferred from replacedErrorHandler on construction
main_activity_flow: YES (message matches connectorConfig.customRetriableException regex) → retriable = true
NO → silently discard (already handling an error)
isRetriable_flow: → for each e in communicationExceptions(): e.isAssignableFrom(throwable.class)? return true
→ walk cause chain: if SQLException AND errorCode in NON_RETRIABLE_ERROR_CODES (1236) → return false
→ fall through to super.isRetriable(throwable) [uses IOException + SQLException as communicationExceptions]
hasMoreRetries_flow: → if doRetry: retries++; log WARN 'Retry N of M'
heartbeat_error_flow: default → no-op (swallow, transient heartbeat issue ignored)
constructor_init_flow: → replacedErrorHandler != null? retries = replacedErrorHandler.getRetries() [preserves retry count across replacements]
binlog_validation_errors: validateBinlogConfiguration(): if shouldStream() and not ROW binlog_format → throw DebeziumException
validateBinlogConfiguration(): if shouldStream() and not FULL binlog_row_image → throw DebeziumException
validateSnapshotFeasibility(): logs WARN if no binlog files or binlogs appear purged (non-throwing)
key_decisions_for_diagram: Is MySql error code in NON_RETRIABLE set (1236)?
Is this the first producer throwable? (CAS guard)
Have max retries been exhausted? (unlimited or counter check)
Heartbeat SQL state: 42000 or 3D000 → fatal; else → ignored
important_notes: MySqlErrorHandler short-circuits isRetriable to false for error code 1236 BEFORE calling super — meaning even if IOException is in the cause chain, a wrapping SQLException(1236) makes it non-retriable. producerThrowable uses AtomicReference CAS to ensure only the first error is processed (thread-safe). RetriableException signals Kafka Connect to restart; ConnectException signals permanent stop.
